sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
UP worked! Squares moved from rows 14-17 to rows 10-13. So vertical movement IS possible when there's a clear black path.
Now the squares are at rows 10-13, cols 18-21 and 42-45.
Let me think about the maze structure:
- Rows 6-13: B at cols 6-29 and 34-57
- Rows 14-17: B at cols 6-17, 22-29, 34-41, 46-57
So at rows 10-13, the squares are in the B region (cols 6-29 for left, 34-57 for right). Good.
Now I need to figure out the goal. Looking at the image, I see:
- A vertical purple path going down through the center (cols 30-33)
- Checkerboard patterns forming paths at rows 38-41 and 54-57
- The pink and purple regions form specific shapes
The squares need to navigate through the black corridors to reach some destination. Let me think about what the goal might be.
In level 1, the goal was to bring both squares together in the middle. Here, maybe the goal is similar but with a more complex maze.
Let me look for black corridors that lead down from rows 6-13. The black corridor map shows:
- Rows 6-13: B at cols 6-29 and 34-57
So from rows 6-13, the squares can move left/right within these regions. But to move down, they need to find a black corridor at rows 14+.
Looking at rows 14-17: B at cols 6-17, 22-29, 34-41, 46-57.
So from rows 6-13, the squares can move down to rows 14-17 if they're at cols 6-17, 22-29, 34-41, or 46-57.
Currently, the squares are at cols 18-21 and 42-45. These are NOT in the black regions at rows 14-17. So the squares can't move down from their current position.
To move down, the squares need to be at cols 6-17, 22-29, 34-41, or 46-57.
The left square is at cols 18-21. To move down, it needs to be at cols 6-17 or 22-29. So it needs to move LEFT to cols 14-17 or RIGHT to cols 22-25.
The right square is at cols 42-45. To move down, it needs to be at cols 34-41 or 46-57. So it needs to move LEFT to cols 38-41 or RIGHT to cols 46-49.
Wait, but the squares move together. So if I press RIGHT, both squares move right. The left square would go to cols 22-25 (in the B region 22-29), and the right square would go to cols 46-49 (in the B region 46-57). Then both squares would be in black regions at rows 14-17, and DOWN would work!
Let me try this: RIGHT then DOWN.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Squares at rows 10-13, cols 18-21 and 42-45
# To move down, need to be in black regions at rows 14-17: cols 6-17, 22-29, 34-41, 46-57
# LEFT square at 18-21 needs to move to 22-25 (RIGHT) or 14-17 (LEFT)
# RIGHT square at 42-45 needs to move to 46-49 (RIGHT) or 38-41 (LEFT)
# If I press RIGHT: left square -> 22-25 (in B region 22-29), right square -> 46-49 (in B region 46-57)
# Then DOWN should work!

predict(expect_change=True, note='RIGHT - move squares to black corridor positions')
action(['RIGHT'])

seg = current_frame.segmentation
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] == 'S':
        rows = [p[0] for p in n['boundary']]
        cols = [p[1] for p in n['boundary']]
        print(f"Blue: rows {min(rows)}-{max(rows)}, cols {min(cols)}-{max(cols)}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

sections.label: TOOL RESULT: python
sections.content:
Blue: rows 10-13, cols 22-25
Blue: rows 10-13, cols 38-41
